import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
print("=== SHAPE ===")
print(df.shape)
print("\n=== DTYPES ===")
print(df.dtypes)
print("\n=== NULLS ===")
print(df.isnull().sum())
print("\n=== BASIC STATS ===")
print(df.describe().to_string())
print("\n=== DATE RANGE ===")
print(f"From {df['date'].min()} to {df['date'].max()}")
print(f"Date gaps: {df['date'].diff().dropna().nunique()} unique diffs")
diffs = df['date'].diff().dropna()
print(f"Min diff: {diffs.min()}, Max diff: {diffs.max()}")
print("\n=== FIRST/LAST 5 ===")
print(df.head().to_string())
print("...")
print(df.tail().to_string())
print("\n=== CORRELATION MATRIX ===")
print(df.drop(columns='date').corr().round(3).to_string())
print("\n=== NEGATIVE / ZERO VALUES ===")
for col in df.columns:
    if col == 'date': continue
    neg = (df[col] < 0).sum()
    zero = (df[col] == 0).sum()
    if neg > 0 or zero > 0:
        print(f"  {col}: {neg} negative, {zero} zero")
print("\n=== OUTLIERS (IQR method) ===")
for col in df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns:
    Q1, Q3 = df[col].quantile([0.25, 0.75])
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower, upper = Q1 - 1.5*IQR, Q3 + 1.5*IQR
    outliers = ((df[col] < lower) | (df[col] > upper)).sum()
    if outliers > 0:
        print(f"  {col}: {outliers} outliers (range [{lower:.1f}, {upper:.1f}])")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import seaborn as sns

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)

# ============================================================
# PLOT 1: Time series overview of all variables
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 2, figsize=(16, 12), sharex=True)
cols = ['pageviews', 'unique_visitors', 'bounce_rate', 'avg_session_duration_sec', 'new_signups', 'support_tickets']
colors = ['#2196F3', '#4CAF50', '#FF9800', '#9C27B0', '#F44336', '#795548']

for ax, col, color in zip(axes.flat, cols, colors):
    ax.plot(df.index, df[col], alpha=0.3, color=color, linewidth=0.5)
    # 30-day rolling mean
    rolling = df[col].rolling(30, center=True).mean()
    ax.plot(df.index, rolling, color=color, linewidth=2, label='30-day MA')
    ax.set_title(col.replace('_', ' ').title(), fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Web Analytics Time Series Overview (2022-2024)', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/01_time_series_overview.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 1 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 2: Correlation heatmap
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
corr = df.corr()
mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, dtype=bool), k=1)
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, fmt='.3f', cmap='RdBu_r', center=0, 
            vmin=-1, vmax=1, ax=ax, square=True)
ax.set_title('Correlation Matrix', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/02_correlation_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 2 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 3: Day-of-week patterns
# ============================================================
df_reset = df.reset_index()
df_reset['dow'] = df_reset['date'].dt.day_name()
dow_order = ['Monday','Tuesday','Wednesday','Thursday','Friday','Saturday','Sunday']

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(16, 10))
for ax, col in zip(axes.flat, cols):
    sns.boxplot(data=df_reset, x='dow', y=col, order=dow_order, ax=ax, palette='Set2')
    ax.set_title(col.replace('_', ' ').title(), fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('')
    ax.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)
plt.suptitle('Day-of-Week Patterns', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/03_day_of_week.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 3 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 4: Monthly trends with decomposition
# ============================================================
from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose

fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(16, 12))
result = seasonal_decompose(df['pageviews'], model='additive', period=7)
result.observed.plot(ax=axes[0], title='Observed', color='#2196F3', alpha=0.6)
result.trend.plot(ax=axes[1], title='Trend', color='#4CAF50')
result.seasonal.plot(ax=axes[2], title='Seasonal (Weekly)', color='#FF9800')
result.resid.plot(ax=axes[3], title='Residual', color='#F44336', alpha=0.5)
for ax in axes:
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.suptitle('Pageviews: Seasonal Decomposition (period=7)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/04_seasonal_decomposition.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 4 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 5: Distributions
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(16, 10))
for ax, col, color in zip(axes.flat, cols, colors):
    ax.hist(df[col], bins=40, color=color, alpha=0.7, edgecolor='white')
    ax.axvline(df[col].mean(), color='black', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean: {df[col].mean():.1f}')
    ax.axvline(df[col].median(), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'Median: {df[col].median():.1f}')
    ax.set_title(col.replace('_', ' ').title(), fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
plt.suptitle('Distributions', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/05_distributions.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 5 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 6: Pageviews vs Unique Visitors scatter
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
scatter = ax.scatter(df['unique_visitors'], df['pageviews'], 
                     c=df.index.to_julian_date(), cmap='viridis', alpha=0.5, s=15)
plt.colorbar(scatter, ax=ax, label='Date')
# Fit line
from numpy.polynomial import polynomial as P
coeffs = np.polyfit(df['unique_visitors'], df['pageviews'], 1)
x_line = np.linspace(df['unique_visitors'].min(), df['unique_visitors'].max(), 100)
ax.plot(x_line, np.polyval(coeffs, x_line), 'r-', linewidth=2, 
        label=f'y = {coeffs[0]:.2f}x + {coeffs[1]:.1f}')
ax.set_xlabel('Unique Visitors')
ax.set_ylabel('Pageviews')
ax.set_title('Pageviews vs Unique Visitors (colored by date)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/06_pageviews_vs_visitors.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 6 done")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)

# ============================================================
# Deeper statistical analysis
# ============================================================
print("=== MONTHLY AGGREGATES ===")
monthly = df.resample('ME').agg({
    'pageviews': 'mean',
    'unique_visitors': 'mean',
    'bounce_rate': 'mean',
    'avg_session_duration_sec': 'mean',
    'new_signups': 'sum',
    'support_tickets': 'sum'
})
print(monthly.to_string())

print("\n=== YEAR-OVER-YEAR GROWTH ===")
yearly = df.resample('YE').agg({
    'pageviews': 'mean',
    'unique_visitors': 'mean',
    'bounce_rate': 'mean',
    'new_signups': 'sum',
    'support_tickets': 'sum'
})
print(yearly.to_string())

# Day-of-week stats
print("\n=== DAY-OF-WEEK AVERAGES ===")
df_r = df.reset_index()
df_r['dow'] = df_r['date'].dt.day_name()
dow_stats = df_r.groupby('dow')[['pageviews','unique_visitors','bounce_rate']].mean()
dow_order = ['Monday','Tuesday','Wednesday','Thursday','Friday','Saturday','Sunday']
print(dow_stats.loc[dow_order].round(1).to_string())

# Pages per visit
df['pages_per_visit'] = df['pageviews'] / df['unique_visitors']
print(f"\n=== PAGES PER VISIT ===")
print(df['pages_per_visit'].describe().round(3))

# Linear trend in pageviews
from scipy import stats
x = np.arange(len(df))
slope, intercept, r_value, p_value, std_err = stats.linregress(x, df['pageviews'])
print(f"\n=== PAGEVIEWS LINEAR TREND ===")
print(f"Slope: {slope:.4f} pageviews/day")
print(f"Per year: {slope*365:.1f} pageviews/year")
print(f"R-squared: {r_value**2:.4f}")
print(f"P-value: {p_value:.2e}")

slope2, intercept2, r2, p2, se2 = stats.linregress(x, df['unique_visitors'])
print(f"\n=== UNIQUE VISITORS LINEAR TREND ===")
print(f"Slope: {slope2:.4f} visitors/day")
print(f"Per year: {slope2*365:.1f} visitors/year")
print(f"R-squared: {r2**2:.4f}")
print(f"P-value: {p2:.2e}")

# Check stationarity of bounce_rate, session_duration, signups, tickets
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller
print("\n=== ADF STATIONARITY TESTS ===")
for col in ['bounce_rate', 'avg_session_duration_sec', 'new_signups', 'support_tickets', 'pageviews']:
    result = adfuller(df[col], autolag='AIC')
    print(f"  {col}: ADF={result[0]:.3f}, p={result[1]:.4f} ({'stationary' if result[1] < 0.05 else 'NON-STATIONARY'})")

# Check if visitor-to-pageview ratio changes over time
print("\n=== PAGES/VISIT TREND ===")
slope3, intercept3, r3, p3, se3 = stats.linregress(x, df['pages_per_visit'])
print(f"Slope: {slope3:.6f}/day, R²: {r3**2:.4f}, p: {p3:.4f}")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)

# ============================================================
# PLOT 7: Monthly averages with trend
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(16, 10))

monthly_pv = df['pageviews'].resample('ME').mean()
monthly_uv = df['unique_visitors'].resample('ME').mean()

axes[0].bar(monthly_pv.index, monthly_pv.values, width=25, color='#2196F3', alpha=0.7)
z = np.polyfit(np.arange(len(monthly_pv)), monthly_pv.values, 1)
axes[0].plot(monthly_pv.index, np.polyval(z, np.arange(len(monthly_pv))), 'r--', linewidth=2, label='Linear trend')
axes[0].set_title('Monthly Average Pageviews', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
axes[0].legend()
axes[0].grid(alpha=0.3)

axes[1].bar(monthly_uv.index, monthly_uv.values, width=25, color='#4CAF50', alpha=0.7)
z2 = np.polyfit(np.arange(len(monthly_uv)), monthly_uv.values, 1)
axes[1].plot(monthly_uv.index, np.polyval(z2, np.arange(len(monthly_uv))), 'r--', linewidth=2, label='Linear trend')
axes[1].set_title('Monthly Average Unique Visitors', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/07_monthly_trends.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 7 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 8: Seasonality pattern (annual cycle visible?)
# ============================================================
# Group by month-of-year across all years
df_r = df.reset_index()
df_r['month'] = df_r['date'].dt.month
df_r['year'] = df_r['date'].dt.year

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
for year in df_r['year'].unique():
    yr_data = df_r[df_r['year']==year].groupby('month')['pageviews'].mean()
    ax.plot(yr_data.index, yr_data.values, 'o-', label=str(year), linewidth=2)
ax.set_xlabel('Month')
ax.set_ylabel('Average Daily Pageviews')
ax.set_title('Annual Seasonality Pattern by Year', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend()
ax.set_xticks(range(1,13))
ax.set_xticklabels(['Jan','Feb','Mar','Apr','May','Jun','Jul','Aug','Sep','Oct','Nov','Dec'])
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/08_annual_seasonality.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 8 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 9: Autocorrelation plots
# ============================================================
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))
plot_acf(df['pageviews'], lags=60, ax=axes[0,0], title='Pageviews ACF')
plot_pacf(df['pageviews'], lags=60, ax=axes[0,1], title='Pageviews PACF')
# Differenced for stationarity
diff_pv = df['pageviews'].diff().dropna()
plot_acf(diff_pv, lags=60, ax=axes[1,0], title='Differenced Pageviews ACF')
plot_pacf(diff_pv, lags=60, ax=axes[1,1], title='Differenced Pageviews PACF')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/09_autocorrelation.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 9 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 10: Visitor-to-pageview ratio over time (pages per visit)
# ============================================================
df['pages_per_visit'] = df['pageviews'] / df['unique_visitors']
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
ax.plot(df.index, df['pages_per_visit'], alpha=0.3, color='#9C27B0')
rolling = df['pages_per_visit'].rolling(30, center=True).mean()
ax.plot(df.index, rolling, color='#9C27B0', linewidth=2, label='30-day MA')
ax.axhline(df['pages_per_visit'].mean(), color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label=f"Mean: {df['pages_per_visit'].mean():.2f}")
ax.set_title('Pages per Visit Over Time', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel('Pages/Visit')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/10_pages_per_visit.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 10 done")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy import stats

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)

# ============================================================
# Deep dive: Annual sinusoidal pattern in pageviews
# ============================================================
# The annual seasonality plot shows a clear cycle peaking around March/April
# and troughing around September/October. Let me quantify this.

# Detrend first
x = np.arange(len(df))
slope, intercept, _, _, _ = stats.linregress(x, df['pageviews'])
detrended = df['pageviews'] - (slope * x + intercept)

# Check for weekly periodicity
from scipy.signal import periodogram
freq, power = periodogram(detrended, fs=1.0)  # daily sampling
top_periods = 1.0 / freq[1:]  # skip zero freq
top_power = power[1:]

# Get top 10 peaks
peak_idx = np.argsort(top_power)[-10:][::-1]
print("=== TOP 10 SPECTRAL PEAKS (detrended pageviews) ===")
for i in peak_idx:
    print(f"  Period: {top_periods[i]:.1f} days, Power: {top_power[i]:.0f}")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 11: Periodogram
# ============================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
ax.semilogy(1.0/freq[1:100], power[1:100], 'b-')
ax.set_xlabel('Period (days)')
ax.set_ylabel('Spectral Power (log scale)')
ax.set_title('Periodogram of Detrended Pageviews', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
ax.axvline(7, color='r', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7, label='7 days (weekly)')
ax.axvline(365.25, color='g', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7, label='365 days (annual)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/11_periodogram.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 11 done")

# ============================================================
# Conversion rate analysis: signups / unique_visitors
# ============================================================
df['conversion_rate'] = df['new_signups'] / df['unique_visitors']
print(f"\n=== CONVERSION RATE ===")
print(df['conversion_rate'].describe().round(5))

slope_cr, _, r_cr, p_cr, _ = stats.linregress(x, df['conversion_rate'])
print(f"Trend slope: {slope_cr:.8f}/day, R²: {r_cr**2:.4f}, p: {p_cr:.4f}")

# Support ticket rate: tickets / unique_visitors
df['ticket_rate'] = df['support_tickets'] / df['unique_visitors']
print(f"\n=== SUPPORT TICKET RATE ===")
print(df['ticket_rate'].describe().round(5))

slope_tr, _, r_tr, p_tr, _ = stats.linregress(x, df['ticket_rate'])
print(f"Trend slope: {slope_tr:.8f}/day, R²: {r_tr**2:.4f}, p: {p_tr:.4f}")

# ============================================================
# Is bounce rate related to any other variable?
# ============================================================
print(f"\n=== BOUNCE RATE CORRELATIONS ===")
print(df[['bounce_rate','pageviews','unique_visitors','avg_session_duration_sec','new_signups','support_tickets']].corr()['bounce_rate'].round(4))

# Regression: what predicts new_signups?
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

features = ['pageviews', 'unique_visitors', 'bounce_rate', 'avg_session_duration_sec']
X = df[features].values
y_signups = df['new_signups'].values

# Standardize for coefficient comparison
scaler = StandardScaler()
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X)
lr = LinearRegression().fit(X_scaled, y_signups)
print(f"\n=== SIGNUPS REGRESSION (standardized coefficients) ===")
print(f"R²: {lr.score(X_scaled, y_signups):.4f}")
for feat, coef in zip(features, lr.coef_):
    print(f"  {feat}: {coef:.4f}")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy import stats
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)
df.index.freq = 'D'

# ============================================================
# Time Series Model: SARIMAX for pageviews
# ============================================================
# Strategy: use last 90 days as test set
train = df['pageviews'][:-90]
test = df['pageviews'][-90:]

# Model 1: Linear trend + Fourier terms (annual + weekly)
# This is a regression approach that captures the clear patterns
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, r2_score

def create_features(dates):
    """Create time series features."""
    x = np.arange(len(dates))
    features = pd.DataFrame(index=dates)
    features['trend'] = x
    # Annual Fourier terms
    day_of_year = dates.dayofyear / 365.25
    for k in [1, 2]:
        features[f'sin_annual_{k}'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_year)
        features[f'cos_annual_{k}'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_year)
    # Weekly Fourier terms  
    day_of_week = dates.dayofweek / 7.0
    for k in [1, 2, 3]:
        features[f'sin_weekly_{k}'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_week)
        features[f'cos_weekly_{k}'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_week)
    return features

# Full feature set
all_dates = df.index
X_all = create_features(all_dates)
X_train = X_all.iloc[:-90]
X_test = X_all.iloc[-90:]
y_train = train.values
y_test = test.values

from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
model_lr = LinearRegression()
model_lr.fit(X_train, y_train)

pred_train = model_lr.predict(X_train)
pred_test = model_lr.predict(X_test)

print("=== LINEAR REGRESSION + FOURIER TERMS ===")
print(f"Train R²: {r2_score(y_train, pred_train):.4f}")
print(f"Train MAE: {mean_absolute_error(y_train, pred_train):.1f}")
print(f"Test R²: {r2_score(y_test, pred_test):.4f}")  
print(f"Test MAE: {mean_absolute_error(y_test, pred_test):.1f}")
print(f"Test RMSE: {np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, pred_test)):.1f}")

# Feature importances
print("\nCoefficients:")
for name, coef in zip(X_train.columns, model_lr.coef_):
    print(f"  {name}: {coef:.4f}")
print(f"  intercept: {model_lr.intercept_:.4f}")

# ============================================================
# Model 2: SARIMAX
# ============================================================
from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX

# Use a simpler SARIMAX with weekly seasonality
# SARIMAX(1,1,1)(1,1,1,7)
print("\n=== FITTING SARIMAX(1,1,1)(1,1,1,7) ===")
model_sarima = SARIMAX(train, order=(1,1,1), seasonal_order=(1,1,1,7), 
                       enforce_stationarity=False, enforce_invertibility=False)
results_sarima = model_sarima.fit(disp=False, maxiter=500)
print(results_sarima.summary().tables[1])

# Forecast
forecast = results_sarima.forecast(steps=90)
print(f"\nSARIMAX Test MAE: {mean_absolute_error(y_test, forecast):.1f}")
print(f"SARIMAX Test RMSE: {np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, forecast)):.1f}")
print(f"SARIMAX Test R²: {r2_score(y_test, forecast):.4f}")
print(f"AIC: {results_sarima.aic:.1f}")
print(f"BIC: {results_sarima.bic:.1f}")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 12: Model comparison - forecast vs actual
# ============================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(16, 10))

# Linear + Fourier
axes[0].plot(train.index[-180:], train.values[-180:], 'b-', alpha=0.5, label='Train')
axes[0].plot(test.index, y_test, 'b-', linewidth=2, label='Actual (test)')
axes[0].plot(test.index, pred_test, 'r--', linewidth=2, label='Predicted')
axes[0].axvline(test.index[0], color='gray', linestyle=':', alpha=0.5)
axes[0].set_title(f'Linear Trend + Fourier (Test MAE={mean_absolute_error(y_test, pred_test):.0f})', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].legend()
axes[0].grid(alpha=0.3)

# SARIMAX
axes[1].plot(train.index[-180:], train.values[-180:], 'b-', alpha=0.5, label='Train')
axes[1].plot(test.index, y_test, 'b-', linewidth=2, label='Actual (test)')
axes[1].plot(test.index, forecast.values, 'r--', linewidth=2, label='Predicted')
axes[1].axvline(test.index[0], color='gray', linestyle=':', alpha=0.5)
axes[1].set_title(f'SARIMAX(1,1,1)(1,1,1,7) (Test MAE={mean_absolute_error(y_test, forecast):.0f})', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Pageviews Forecasting: Model Comparison', fontsize=15, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/12_model_comparison.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 12 done")

# ============================================================
# Residual diagnostics for the better model
# ============================================================
residuals_lr = y_train - pred_train

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))

# Residuals over time
axes[0,0].plot(train.index, residuals_lr, alpha=0.5, color='steelblue')
axes[0,0].axhline(0, color='red', linestyle='--')
axes[0,0].set_title('Residuals Over Time', fontweight='bold')
axes[0,0].grid(alpha=0.3)

# Residual distribution
axes[0,1].hist(residuals_lr, bins=50, color='steelblue', alpha=0.7, edgecolor='white', density=True)
xr = np.linspace(residuals_lr.min(), residuals_lr.max(), 100)
axes[0,1].plot(xr, stats.norm.pdf(xr, residuals_lr.mean(), residuals_lr.std()), 'r-', linewidth=2)
axes[0,1].set_title(f'Residual Distribution (Shapiro p={stats.shapiro(residuals_lr[:500])[1]:.4f})', fontweight='bold')

# QQ plot
stats.probplot(residuals_lr, dist="norm", plot=axes[1,0])
axes[1,0].set_title('Q-Q Plot', fontweight='bold')

# Residuals vs predicted
axes[1,1].scatter(pred_train, residuals_lr, alpha=0.3, s=10, color='steelblue')
axes[1,1].axhline(0, color='red', linestyle='--')
axes[1,1].set_xlabel('Predicted')
axes[1,1].set_ylabel('Residual')
axes[1,1].set_title('Residuals vs Predicted', fontweight='bold')
axes[1,1].grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Linear+Fourier Model: Residual Diagnostics', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/13_residual_diagnostics.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 13 done")

# Heteroscedasticity test
from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import het_breuschpagan
import statsmodels.api as sm
X_train_sm = sm.add_constant(X_train)
ols_model = sm.OLS(y_train, X_train_sm).fit()
bp_test = het_breuschpagan(ols_model.resid, X_train_sm)
print(f"\nBreusch-Pagan test: stat={bp_test[0]:.2f}, p={bp_test[1]:.4f}")

# Durbin-Watson
from statsmodels.stats.stattools import durbin_watson
dw = durbin_watson(residuals_lr)
print(f"Durbin-Watson: {dw:.4f} (2.0 = no autocorrelation)")

# Ljung-Box on residuals
from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import acorr_ljungbox
lb = acorr_ljungbox(residuals_lr, lags=[7, 14, 30], return_df=True)
print(f"\nLjung-Box test on residuals:")
print(lb.to_string())

PYEOF
tool_input.description: Build and evaluate time series models

tool_input.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import stats

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv', parse_dates=['date'])
df.set_index('date', inplace=True)

# ============================================================
# PLOT 14: Conversion & ticket rates over time  
# ============================================================
df['conversion_rate'] = df['new_signups'] / df['unique_visitors'] * 100
df['ticket_rate'] = df['support_tickets'] / df['unique_visitors'] * 100

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(16, 8))

ax = axes[0]
ax.plot(df.index, df['conversion_rate'], alpha=0.2, color='#4CAF50')
roll = df['conversion_rate'].rolling(30, center=True).mean()
ax.plot(df.index, roll, color='#4CAF50', linewidth=2, label='30-day MA')
ax.set_title('Signup Conversion Rate (signups/visitors)', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_ylabel('Conversion Rate (%)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

ax = axes[1]
ax.plot(df.index, df['ticket_rate'], alpha=0.2, color='#F44336')
roll2 = df['ticket_rate'].rolling(30, center=True).mean()
ax.plot(df.index, roll2, color='#F44336', linewidth=2, label='30-day MA')
ax.set_title('Support Ticket Rate (tickets/visitors)', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_ylabel('Ticket Rate (%)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/14_rate_metrics.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 14 done")

# ============================================================
# PLOT 15: Full model fit + forecast visualization
# ============================================================
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, r2_score

def create_features(dates):
    x = (dates - dates[0]).days if hasattr(dates[0], 'day') else np.arange(len(dates))
    features = pd.DataFrame(index=dates)
    features['trend'] = np.arange(len(dates))
    day_of_year = dates.dayofyear / 365.25
    for k in [1, 2]:
        features[f'sin_annual_{k}'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_year)
        features[f'cos_annual_{k}'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_year)
    day_of_week = dates.dayofweek / 7.0
    for k in [1, 2, 3]:
        features[f'sin_weekly_{k}'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_week)
        features[f'cos_weekly_{k}'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * k * day_of_week)
    return features

X_all = create_features(df.index)
model = LinearRegression().fit(X_all, df['pageviews'])
fitted = model.predict(X_all)

# Forecast 90 days ahead
future_dates = pd.date_range(df.index[-1] + pd.Timedelta(days=1), periods=90, freq='D')
X_future = create_features(future_dates)
X_future['trend'] = np.arange(len(df), len(df)+90)
forecast = model.predict(X_future)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(18, 7))
ax.plot(df.index, df['pageviews'], alpha=0.3, color='#2196F3', label='Actual')
ax.plot(df.index, fitted, color='#F44336', linewidth=1.5, label='Model fit', alpha=0.8)
ax.plot(future_dates, forecast, color='#4CAF50', linewidth=2, linestyle='--', label='90-day forecast')
ax.fill_between(future_dates, forecast - 2*75.6, forecast + 2*75.6, alpha=0.15, color='#4CAF50', label='95% CI')
ax.axvline(df.index[-1], color='gray', linestyle=':', alpha=0.5)
ax.set_title('Pageviews: Full Model Fit + 90-Day Forecast', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_ylabel('Pageviews')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/15_full_model_forecast.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 15 done")

# ============================================================
# Summary statistics for the report
# ============================================================
print("\n=== YEAR-OVER-YEAR SUMMARY ===")
df_r = df.reset_index()
df_r['year'] = df_r['date'].dt.year
for year in sorted(df_r['year'].unique()):
    yr = df_r[df_r['year']==year]
    print(f"\n{year}:")
    print(f"  Avg daily pageviews: {yr['pageviews'].mean():.0f}")
    print(f"  Avg daily visitors: {yr['unique_visitors'].mean():.0f}")
    print(f"  Total signups: {yr['new_signups'].sum()}")
    print(f"  Total tickets: {yr['support_tickets'].sum()}")
    print(f"  Avg bounce rate: {yr['bounce_rate'].mean():.3f}")
    print(f"  Avg session duration: {yr['avg_session_duration_sec'].mean():.0f}s")
    print(f"  Avg conversion rate: {(yr['new_signups']/yr['unique_visitors']).mean()*100:.2f}%")

# Cross-validation for model robustness
from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit
tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5)
scores = []
for train_idx, test_idx in tscv.split(X_all):
    X_tr, X_te = X_all.iloc[train_idx], X_all.iloc[test_idx]
    y_tr, y_te = df['pageviews'].values[train_idx], df['pageviews'].values[test_idx]
    m = LinearRegression().fit(X_tr, y_tr)
    scores.append(r2_score(y_te, m.predict(X_te)))
print(f"\n=== TIME SERIES CV R² SCORES ===")
print(f"Scores: {[f'{s:.4f}' for s in scores]}")
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(scores):.4f}, Std: {np.std(scores):.4f}")

PYEOF

tool_input.content:
# Web Analytics Dataset: Comprehensive Analysis Report

## 1. Dataset Overview

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| **Rows** | 1,095 |
| **Date range** | 2022-01-01 to 2024-12-30 (3 years, daily) |
| **Missing values** | 0 across all columns |
| **Date gaps** | None (consecutive daily observations) |

**Columns:**

| Column | Type | Mean | Std | Min | Max |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| pageviews | int | 940 | 365 | 100 | 1,864 |
| unique_visitors | int | 611 | 245 | 57 | 1,247 |
| bounce_rate | float | 0.448 | 0.078 | 0.172 | 0.695 |
| avg_session_duration_sec | int | 180 | 40 | 64 | 291 |
| new_signups | int | 14.8 | 3.9 | 5 | 27 |
| support_tickets | int | 3.0 | 1.7 | 0 | 10 |

The data is clean: no nulls, no negative values, no impossible ranges. 63 days had zero support tickets, which is plausible.

---

## 2. Key Findings

### 2.1 Strong Growth in Traffic, Flat Engagement Metrics

The dominant pattern in this dataset is **sustained linear growth in traffic** (pageviews and unique visitors) while all engagement and conversion metrics remain stationary.

| Metric | 2022 | 2023 | 2024 | Trend |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Avg daily pageviews | 647 | 934 | 1,240 | +211/year |
| Avg daily visitors | 420 | 608 | 806 | +138/year |
| Total annual signups | 5,444 | 5,328 | 5,475 | Flat (~5,400/year) |
| Total annual tickets | 1,074 | 1,068 | 1,101 | Flat (~1,080/year) |
| Avg bounce rate | 0.445 | 0.453 | 0.445 | Flat (~0.45) |
| Avg session duration | 181s | 178s | 181s | Flat (~180s) |

**Interpretation:** Traffic nearly doubled over 3 years, but signups and support tickets did not grow proportionally. This means the **conversion rate is declining** (4.77% in 2022 -> 2.66% in 2023 -> 2.00% in 2024). The additional traffic is not converting.

### 2.2 Dual Seasonality: Annual + Weekly Cycles

Spectral analysis reveals two dominant periodic patterns in pageviews:

1. **Annual cycle (365-day period):** Pageviews peak around March-April and trough around August-September. The amplitude is approximately +/-400 pageviews around the trend. This pattern repeats consistently across all three years.

2. **Weekly cycle (7-day period):** Mondays and Sundays receive the most traffic (avg ~1,100 pageviews); Thursdays receive the least (avg ~748). This pattern is stable over time.

ADF stationarity tests confirm:
- Pageviews are **non-stationary** (ADF p=0.59) due to the trend
- All other metrics are **stationary** (ADF p<0.0001)

### 2.3 Near-Perfect Coupling Between Pageviews and Visitors

The correlation between pageviews and unique visitors is **0.972**. The pages-per-visit ratio is remarkably stable at 1.55 +/- 0.14 with no trend (slope p=0.94). This means visitor behavior hasn't changed: each visitor views roughly the same number of pages regardless of traffic level.

### 2.4 Independence of Other Metrics

A key finding is that bounce rate, session duration, signups, and support tickets are **essentially uncorrelated** with traffic and with each other (all correlations < 0.05). A regression of signups on all traffic features yields R²=0.002, confirming signups are independent of traffic volume.

This suggests:
- Signups are driven by factors not captured in this dataset (marketing campaigns, product changes, etc.)
- Bounce rate appears to be random noise around ~0.45 with no discernible pattern
- Support tickets are similarly independent of traffic volume

---

## 3. Modeling

### 3.1 Model Selection

Given the clear trend + dual seasonality in pageviews, two models were compared:

1. **Linear Regression + Fourier Terms** (trend + annual harmonics + weekly harmonics, 12 parameters)
2. **SARIMAX(1,1,1)(1,1,1,7)** (seasonal ARIMA with weekly seasonality)

### 3.2 Results (90-day holdout test set)

| Model | Train R² | Test R² | Test MAE | Test RMSE |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Linear + Fourier | 0.957 | 0.873 | 60.1 | 75.6 |
| SARIMAX | -- | -0.135 | 178.8 | 225.7 |

The **Linear + Fourier model is the clear winner**. SARIMAX fails on multi-step forecasting because the annual cycle cannot be captured within a weekly seasonal period.

### 3.3 Model Equation

```
pageviews = 497.3 + 0.81*t + 399.3*sin(2*pi*t_year) - 11.3*cos(2*pi*t_year)
            - 49.5*sin(2*pi*t_week) + 190.7*cos(2*pi*t_week) + noise
```

Where `t` is days from start, `t_year` is fractional day-of-year, `t_week` is fractional day-of-week. The dominant terms are the linear trend (+0.81/day) and the first annual harmonic (amplitude ~399).

### 3.4 Model Diagnostics

All residual diagnostics pass:

| Test | Result | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Breusch-Pagan | p=0.876 | No heteroscedasticity |
| Durbin-Watson | 1.994 | No autocorrelation (2.0 = perfect) |
| Ljung-Box (lag 30) | p=0.934 | No residual autocorrelation |
| Residual normality | Approximately normal | Q-Q plot confirms |

The residuals show no remaining structure, no trend, and constant variance. The model captures all systematic patterns in the data.

### 3.5 Time Series Cross-Validation

Using 5-fold TimeSeriesSplit, the model achieves R² of 0.83-0.86 on all folds except the first (which trains on insufficient data to learn the annual cycle). This confirms the model generalizes well across different time periods.

---

## 4. Anomalies and Data Quality

- **Outliers:** Minimal. Only 5 bounce-rate outliers and 4 session-duration outliers by IQR method. No evidence of data corruption or measurement errors.
- **No structural breaks:** The trend is linear throughout — no sudden shifts in traffic patterns.
- **Pages-per-visit ratio stability:** Extraordinarily stable at 1.55, suggesting consistent user behavior or possibly an artifact of how pageviews are counted.

---

## 5. Business Implications and Recommendations

### 5.1 The Conversion Problem

This is the most actionable finding: **traffic is growing but conversions are not**. The signup conversion rate has halved from 4.8% to 2.0% over 3 years while absolute signups remain flat at ~15/day. This suggests:

- The additional traffic may be lower-intent (e.g., organic search visitors who browse but don't convert)
- The signup funnel may not be optimized for the changing visitor profile
- Marketing/acquisition channels driving growth may differ from those that historically drove conversions

**Recommendation:** Segment visitors by acquisition channel to identify which traffic sources convert and which don't. Consider whether the signup flow needs adjustment for the evolving audience.

### 5.2 Seasonal Planning

The annual cycle is strong and predictable (amplitude ~400 pageviews). Peak traffic occurs in Q1 (March-April) and minimum traffic in Q3 (August-September).

**Recommendation:** Align product launches, campaigns, and infrastructure scaling with the seasonal cycle. Q1 is optimal for conversion experiments due to higher traffic volume.

### 5.3 Day-of-Week Patterns

Monday and Sunday are the highest-traffic days; Thursday is the lowest. This is consistent across all years.

**Recommendation:** Schedule maintenance and deployments for Thursdays. Prioritize content/feature launches for Mondays.

### 5.4 Forecast

Assuming the linear trend and seasonal patterns continue, the model projects:
- Average daily pageviews in 2025: ~1,500-1,600
- Peak daily pageviews (March 2025): ~1,800-2,000
- Trough daily pageviews (September 2025): ~1,100-1,300

The 95% confidence interval is approximately +/- 150 pageviews around the point forecast.

---

## 6. Plots Reference

| Plot | Description |
|---|---|
| `01_time_series_overview.png` | All 6 metrics with 30-day moving averages |
| `02_correlation_matrix.png` | Pairwise correlations |
| `03_day_of_week.png` | Boxplots by day of week |
| `04_seasonal_decomposition.png` | Additive decomposition (trend, seasonal, residual) |
| `05_distributions.png` | Histograms with mean/median |
| `06_pageviews_vs_visitors.png` | Scatter plot colored by date |
| `07_monthly_trends.png` | Monthly aggregates with linear trend |
| `08_annual_seasonality.png` | Year-over-year monthly comparison |
| `09_autocorrelation.png` | ACF/PACF for raw and differenced pageviews |
| `10_pages_per_visit.png` | Engagement ratio stability |
| `11_periodogram.png` | Spectral analysis showing 365-day and 7-day peaks |
| `12_model_comparison.png` | Linear+Fourier vs SARIMAX on test set |
| `13_residual_diagnostics.png` | Residual plots, Q-Q, heteroscedasticity check |
| `14_rate_metrics.png` | Conversion rate and ticket rate over time |
| `15_full_model_forecast.png` | Model fit with 90-day forecast and confidence interval |

---

## 7. Methodology Notes

- **Stationarity:** Tested with Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF)
- **Spectral analysis:** Periodogram on detrended pageviews
- **Modeling:** Compared parametric (Linear + Fourier) and ARIMA-based (SARIMAX) approaches
- **Validation:** 90-day holdout + 5-fold time series cross-validation
- **Residual diagnostics:** Breusch-Pagan (heteroscedasticity), Durbin-Watson (autocorrelation), Ljung-Box (serial correlation), Q-Q plot (normality)
- **All analysis performed in Python** using pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels, scikit-learn, matplotlib, and seaborn
